checks.label: Primary keyword appears in the page title or H1
checks.verification_method: Look at article.title or the first <h1> and compare it to request.primary_keyword.
checks.evidence: Current title/H1 is "What Is Answer Engine Optimization (AEO)? The Complete 2026 Definition" and it does not include "what is aeo".
checks.exact_fix: Change the title to include the primary keyword, for example: "what is aeo | What Is Answer Engine Optimization (AEO)? The Complete 2026 Definition".

checks.label: Meta title is keyword-aligned and within 45-65 characters
checks.verification_method: Count article.meta_title characters and verify it includes request.primary_keyword or a close variant.
checks.evidence: Meta title is 55 characters and keyword-aligned.

checks.label: Meta description is specific and within 140-165 characters
checks.verification_method: Count article.meta_description characters and check for concrete nouns or numbers, not empty hype.
checks.evidence: Meta description is 136 characters: "What is AEO? Learn the 2026 answer engine optimization (AEO) definition, how it compares to SEO, and why AEO, GEO & LLMO are converging.".
checks.exact_fix: Replace meta_description with a 140-165 character summary that names the topic, outcome, and one concrete detail.

checks.label: Slug is concise and keyword-relevant
checks.verification_method: Check article.slug against request.primary_keyword and keep it under 80 characters.
checks.evidence: Slug "answer-engine-optimization" is either too long or not aligned to the primary keyword.
checks.exact_fix: Replace slug with a short keyword-led slug such as "what-is-aeo".

checks.label: The article body has enough topical depth
checks.verification_method: Count section blocks and total visible words across article text or HTML.
checks.evidence: Detected 9 sections and about 1010 words.

checks.label: The post includes a fast-answer block near the top
checks.verification_method: Check article.quick_answer or an equivalent early answer block in the first 20% of HTML.
checks.evidence: Article includes 3 quick-answer bullets.

checks.label: Headings are question-led or snippet-friendly
checks.verification_method: Review section headings for question, comparison, or answer-led phrasing.
checks.evidence: 4 of 9 headings are answer-oriented.

checks.label: The post includes reader-retention elements
checks.verification_method: Check for key takeaways, summary box, bullets, FAQ, or comparison structures.
checks.evidence: Detected retention elements: key_takeaways, summary_box, faq, bullets.

checks.label: Paragraphs are concise enough for AI extraction
checks.verification_method:
Measure average paragraph length in article paragraphs or HTML

tags.
checks.evidence: Average paragraph length is about 38 words.

checks.label: A clear next step or CTA is present
checks.verification_method: Check article.call_to_action or a dedicated CTA block in HTML.
checks.evidence: CTA detected with label "Measure your AEO visibility".

checks.label: HTML contains one semantic <article> root and exactly one <h1>
checks.verification_method: Count <article> and <h1> tags in the HTML.
checks.evidence: Detected 4 <article> tags and 1 <h1> tags.
checks.exact_fix: Render a single `<article>` wrapper and keep exactly one `<h1>` for the page title.

checks.label: Heading hierarchy is clean
checks.verification_method: Verify the page starts with <h1>, uses <h2> for main sections, and does not jump to <h4+>.
checks.evidence: Heading order starts with h1 and avoids deep heading jumps.

checks.label: Inline citation anchors resolve to reference targets
checks.verification_method: Compare href="#source-*" anchors against matching id attributes in the references list.
checks.evidence: All 35 citation anchors resolve.

checks.label: Primary keyword and intent are embedded as machine-readable HTML attributes
checks.verification_method: Look for data-primary-keyword and data-search-intent on the root article element.
checks.evidence: Root article element includes both keyword and intent attributes.

checks.label: HTML includes an embedded JSON-LD script
checks.verification_method: Look for <script type="application/ld+json"> in the rendered HTML.
checks.evidence: Embedded JSON-LD script tag detected.

checks.label: JSON-LD includes a BlogPosting node
checks.verification_method: Inspect the JSON-LD graph for @type BlogPosting.
checks.evidence: BlogPosting node detected.

checks.label: Core BlogPosting fields are complete
checks.verification_method: Verify the BlogPosting node has headline, description, url, author, publisher, datePublished, and dateModified.
checks.evidence: All core BlogPosting fields are present.

checks.label: FAQ schema is present when the article contains FAQs
checks.verification_method: If article.faq has items, verify a FAQPage node exists in JSON-LD.
checks.evidence: FAQPage node detected.

checks.label: Evidence sources are modeled as cited entities in JSON-LD
checks.verification_method: Check for citation or cited CreativeWork nodes tied to the BlogPosting.
checks.evidence: Detected 5 CreativeWork source nodes and 5 citation links.

checks.label: JSON-LD names the main entities or topics explicitly
checks.verification_method: Check BlogPosting for about or mentions arrays with meaningful entity strings or typed nodes.
checks.evidence: Detected about=4 and mentions=5.

checks.label: Format-specific schema is present when the format requires it
checks.verification_method: Require HowTo schema for how-to posts and BreadcrumbList when breadcrumbs are supplied.
checks.evidence: HowTo required=false present=false; BreadcrumbList required=true present=true.

checks.label: Factual paragraphs are source-backed
checks.verification_method: Check citation_ids on intro, section paragraphs, FAQ answers, and conclusion paragraphs.
checks.evidence: 7 of 18 narrative blocks have no citation_ids.
checks.exact_fix: Add at least one valid `citation_id` to every factual intro, body, FAQ, and conclusion paragraph.

checks.label: Provided sources are actually used
checks.verification_method: Compare references or request.source_count against cited source ids and validation.uncited_source_ids.
checks.evidence: All provided sources are cited at least once.

checks.label: The article contains concrete specifics, not just generic claims
checks.verification_method: Look for numbers, dates, prices, percentages, or named frameworks in article text or HTML.
checks.evidence: Detected at least one concrete statistic, date, price, or framework cue.

checks.label: Brand mentions feel integrated rather than stuffed
checks.verification_method: Count brand-name mentions in article text and compare them to total word count.
checks.evidence: Brand density is 0.47% (4 mentions across 851 words).

checks.label: The article closes with a synthesized conclusion
checks.verification_method: Check for at least one conclusion paragraph that summarizes the payoff, not just a CTA.
checks.evidence: Detected 1 conclusion paragraph(s).

checks.label: Author is a Person entity with sameAs LinkedIn URL
checks.verification_method: Check JSON-LD BlogPosting.author for Person with sameAs containing linkedin.com.
checks.evidence: Person author with LinkedIn sameAs detected.

checks.label: Article contains first-party data signals
checks.verification_method: Look for 'we found', 'our data', 'in our audit', 'we tested' + specific numbers.
checks.evidence: Both first-party markers and specific numeric data detected.

checks.label: Article cites named brands/products with specifics (not generic)
checks.verification_method: Look for proper-noun brand names + specific metrics; flag 'leading companies', 'top brands', 'many companies'.
checks.evidence: 44 distinct named entities detected; no generic company language.

checks.label: Article takes a clear editorial stance
checks.verification_method: Look for an opinionated claim, 'our view', 'we argue', 'the better pattern', or a visible Our-Take block.
checks.evidence: Editorial stance banner is present.

checks.label: Article includes an original visual (screenshot, diagram, chart)
checks.verification_method: Look for original-visual markers in HTML (not the hero image or stock photos).
checks.evidence: 2 original visual(s) embedded.

checks.label: 3+ primary-tier citations are present
checks.verification_method: Count references with authority_tier='primary' and verify they're cited in the body.
checks.evidence: 4 primary-tier sources provided.

checks.label: Article has visible Last Reviewed + Next Review dates
checks.verification_method: Look for 'Last reviewed' / 'Next review' timestamps in the rendered HTML byline area.
checks.evidence: Visible Last Reviewed + Next Review + dateModified schema present.

checks.label: No banned AI-tell phrases in the body
checks.verification_method: Scan for filler/hedge phrases that signal unreviewed AI output.
checks.evidence: No banned AI-tell phrases detected.

fix_summary.issue: Primary keyword appears in the page title or H1
fix_summary.exact_fix: Change the title to include the primary keyword, for example: "what is aeo | What Is Answer Engine Optimization (AEO)? The Complete 2026 Definition".

fix_summary.issue: Meta description is specific and within 140-165 characters
fix_summary.exact_fix: Replace meta_description with a 140-165 character summary that names the topic, outcome, and one concrete detail.
Replace slug with a short keyword-led slug such as "what-is-aeo".

fix_summary.issue: HTML contains one semantic <article> root and exactly one <h1>
fix_summary.exact_fix: Render a single `<article>` wrapper and keep exactly one `<h1>` for the page title.
Add at least one valid `citation_id` to every factual intro, body, FAQ, and conclusion paragraph.
